achievementContents.title: KSV Researchers' Case Study Selected for International AI Casebook at IndiaAI Impact Summit 2026
achievementContents.description: Kadi Sarva Vishwavidyalaya (KSV), Gandhinagar, marked a proud moment on Day 2 of the IndiaAI Impact Summit 2026, organized under the Digital India initiative by MeitY, Government of India, where the Compendium "Casebook on AI and Gender Empowerment" was officially launched in collaboration with UN Women. Among hundreds of global submissions, the Students Research Lab (SRL) members of KSV Student Researchers Team—Janki Chitroda, Yashvi Chavda, and Krishna Bhatt (CE, LDRP-ITR, KSV), guided by Dr. Himani Trivedi—were selected among the 23 research works published, earning international recognition. The team also engaged with eminent global leaders and policymakers on inclusive AI innovation, while Dr. Rupesh Vyas and Dr. Nitin Pandya represented KSV at the summit, highlighting the university's strong academic leadership and expanding global research footprint 🌍✨.

achievementContents.title: KSV Students Selected for International AI & Gender Empowerment Casebook at IndiaAI Impact Summit 2026
achievementContents.description: Kadi Sarva Vishwavidyalaya (KSV), Gandhinagar, proudly highlights its strong presence at the IndiaAI Impact Summit 2026, organized under the Digital India initiative by MeitY, Government of India. The Students Research Lab (SRL) members of the KSV Students Researchers Team—Janki Chitroda, Yashvi Chavda, and Krishna Bhatt (CE, LDRP-ITR, KSV), guided by Dr. Himani Trivedi—have been selected for the prestigious Casebook on AI and Gender Empowerment in collaboration with UN Women India, earning national recognition and the opportunity to present their work to a global audience of policymakers and innovators. Representing the university at the summit were Dr. Rupesh Vyas and Dr. Nitin Pandya, reflecting KSV's strong commitment to research excellence, inclusive AI innovation, and impactful global engagement 🌍✨.

achievementContents.title: KSV Students Publish 'TrafficEye' Research in IEEE Xplore at AIMV-2025
achievementContents.description: A proud research achievement for the M. M. Patel Students Research Project Cell – Students Research Lab (SRL) as student researchers successfully published their paper titled "TrafficEye: Intelligent Traffic Optimization Using Deep Learning Approach" in the IEEE Xplore Digital Library through the 2nd IEEE International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Machine Vision (AIMV-2025). Guided by Dr. Himani Trivedi, the research explores the application of deep learning techniques to improve traffic management and optimization, contributing to smarter and more efficient urban mobility solutions.

achievementContents.title: Team BlackBox Secures 4th Runner-Up Position at National-Level INNOVAITION Hackathon
achievementContents.description: Congratulations to Students Research Lab (SRL) members Team BlackBox from Kadi Sarva Vishwavidyalaya (KSV), Gandhinagar, for securing the 4th Runner-Up position at the national-level hackathon INNOVAITION – Shaping Future Innovators, powered by Intuitive.ai in collaboration with Dhirubhai Ambani University. Among 75 shortlisted teams nationwide, the team's performance reflected strong technical excellence, innovation, and collaborative problem-solving. Team members—Kandarp Gajjar, Nancy Patel, Hemant Pande, Charmi Padh, and Honey Modha (LDRP-ITR)—worked under the guidance of Dr. Himani Trivedi, exemplifying the research-driven culture and innovation mindset fostered at the M. M. Patel Students Research Project Cell, KSV Student Research Lab (SRL) 🔬✨.

achievementContents.title: KSV Students Receive Conference Grant for Presenting AI Research at AAMLAD-2025
achievementContents.description: We proudly congratulate the Students Research Lab (SRL) members—Henit Panchal (Presenter), Hetvi Hinsu, and Heny Patel (CE, LDRP-ITR, KSV)—for receiving the Conference Paper Presentation Grant from the M. M. Patel Students Research Project Cell, KSV under the guidance of Dr. Shivani A. Trivedi and Dr. Himani Trivedi. Their paper, "Generative AI as a Catalyst in Indian Education Ecosystems," was presented at AAMLAD-2025 – 1st International Workshop on Advancing AI and ML Across Disciplines, organized by ABV-IIITM, Gwalior and published in Springer (CCIS). This achievement reflects their strong research commitment and contribution to AI-driven educational innovation—heartiest congratulations to the team on this milestone 🚀✨.

achievementContents.title: KSV Students Publish AI Research in IEEE Xplore at AISTS 2025 International Conference
achievementContents.description: A proud research milestone for the M. M. Patel Students Research Project Cell – Students Research Lab (SRL) as student researchers successfully published their paper at the International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Smart Technologies for Sustainability (AISTS) 2025, organized by Marwadi University, Rajkot. The research paper titled "Exploring AI's Influence on Human Thinking: Productivity, Learning, and Cognitive Skills in the Age of XAI" has been published in the IEEE Xplore Digital Library, reflecting the students' dedication to meaningful research and innovation.

achievementContents.title: KSV Researchers Present Sustainability and AI Research at 9th NYC Green School Conference 2025
achievementContents.description: A proud moment for the M. M. Patel Students Research Project Cell – Students Research Lab (SRL) as faculty and student researchers showcased their work at the 9th NYC Green School Conference 2025, organized by Green Mentors at the ILR School, Cornell University, New York. Representing LDRP Institute of Technology & Research, the researchers presented innovative work focused on sustainable construction, ethical AI in education, and AI-driven sustainability diagnostics for smart institutions. Their participation reflects the growing research culture and commitment to addressing global challenges through innovation and interdisciplinary thinking.

achievementContents.title: KSV Student Teams Selected for Finale of National AI Poster Competition by GTU
achievementContents.description: A proud moment for the M. M. Patel Students Research Project Cell – Students Research Lab (SRL) as student researchers have been selected for the finale of the National Poster Competition on AI Powered Research and Innovation, organized by Gujarat Technological University (GTU), Ahmedabad. The selected teams will present their innovative research posters at the national-level finale scheduled for 19–20 September 2025, showcasing their work on AI-driven student dropout analysis using machine learning and air pollution tracking through satellite and ground data fusion. Guided by Dr. Himani Trivedi, this achievement reflects the growing research culture, innovation mindset, and collaborative learning environment fostered within the Students Research Lab.

achievementContents.title: OSMAR Project Shines with Second Prize at Gujarat Innovation Showcase
achievementContents.description: M. M. Patel Students Research Project Cell, KSV proudly congratulates Team OSMAR for securing 🥈 Second Prize (Booth Track) at the Gujarat Innovation Project Showcase held at GEC Surat, Gujarat, organized by Edunet Foundation with support from Shell India Markets Pvt. Ltd. The team presented "OSMAR: Oil Spill Monitoring using Advanced SAR Imagery," effectively demonstrating their hardware–software solution for efficient oil spill detection. Facilitated through the Skills4Future Program under the KSV–Edunet MoU, this achievement reflects the impact of structured mentorship and industry exposure. The team—Pavan Thakkar, Zenisha Devani, Krisha P., Henit Panchal, and Hetvi Hinsu—worked under the guidance of Mr. Rohit Bhadauriya and Dr. Himani Trivedi, showcasing the strong culture of innovation and applied research nurtured at KSV 🚀💡.

achievementContents.title: KSV Student Teams Showcase Projects at National-Level DST–TEC PAG Meeting 2026
achievementContents.description: A proud moment for M. M. Patel Research Cell, KSV and M. M. Patel Students Research Project Cell, KSV, as Students Research Lab (SRL) members represented the university at the National-level 3rd Project Advisor Group (PAG) Meeting of DST–Technology Enabling Centre (DST-TEC) held at Pandit Deendayal Energy University, Gandhinagar. The event, graced by Dr. Krishna Kanth Pulicherla, featured KSV project teams—Henit Panchal, Hetvi Hinsu, Mihir Patel, and Kavya Chandegara—who showcased innovative, technically strong, and real-world relevant solutions. Their participation highlights Kadi Sarva Vishwavidyalaya's growing research ecosystem and its commitment to fostering student-driven innovation and national-level impact ✨🚀.

achievementContents.title: 8 Teams involving 32 students shortlisted for Phase-2 of the AISEHack National Hackathon
achievementContents.description:
Artificial Intelligence for Science and Engineering Hackathon (AISEHack): Innovation, Collaboration & Impact

We are proud to share the participation of students from the Students Research Lab (SRL), M. M. Patel Students Research Project Cell, KSV (MMPSRPC), Kadi Sarva Vishwavidyalaya (KSV) in AISE Hackathon, organised by Anusandhan National Research Foundation (ANRF), IBM, Indian Institute of Technology, Delhi, International Institute of Information Technology Hyderabad (IIITH) Hyderabad, and E-Cell IIITH.

8 teams involving 32 students from Students Research Lab (SRL), M.M. Patel Students Research Project Cell (MMPSRPC), Kadi Sarva Vishwavidyalaya (KSV) are shortlisted for Phase-2 of the hackathon, showcasing their talent, dedication, and innovative thinking.

achievementContents.title: Team Qubits – Runner-Up at Odoo × Gujarat Vidyapith Hackathon 2026
achievementContents.description:
Proud to share that Team Qubits, a team from Students Research Lab (SRL), M. M. Patel Students Research Project Cell, KSV secured the Runner-Up position at the national-level Odoo × Gujarat Vidyapith Hackathon 2026, competing among teams from across the country.

Out of 2300+ registrations nationwide, 239 finalists across 68 teams advanced to the grand finale held on 21st and 22nd March 2026 at Gujarat Vidyapith, Ahmedabad.

Demonstrating exceptional problem-solving skills and collaboration, Team Qubits secured the Runner-Up position along with a cash prize of ₹35,000. The team comprised Kandarp Gajjar (CE, LDRP ITR, KSV), Ridham Patel (IT, LDRP ITR, KSV), and Harsh Dodiya (CE, LDRP ITR, KSV).

This achievement is a testament to their dedication, innovation, and strong team spirit. It also highlights the growing culture of excellence and technical capability fostered at Students Research Lab (SRL), M. M. Patel Students Research Project Cell, and Kadi Sarva Vishwavidyalaya (KSV).

achievementContents.title: 7 KSV Teams Shortlisted Among Top 75 at National-Level INNOVAITION Hackathon
achievementContents.description: A proud moment for the MMPSRPC Students Research Lab (SRL) as 7 student teams have been shortlisted at "INNOVAITION – Shaping Future Innovators," a national-level hackathon powered by Intuitive.ai in collaboration with Dhirubhai Ambani University (DAU).

achievementContents.title: KSV Students Receive Conference Grant for IEEE Paper Presentation at ISED 2025
achievementContents.description: We proudly congratulate the Students Research Lab (SRL) members—Ayushi Joddha (Presenter), Manasvi Shah, and Swayam Kalburgi (CE, LDRP-ITR, KSV)—for receiving the Conference Paper Presentation Grant from the M. M. Patel Students Research Project Cell, KSV under the guidance of Dr. Himani Trivedi. Their paper, "EfficientNetB3 Adapted Hybrid UNet with Attention Guided Decoding for Urban Scene Segmentation," was presented at the 13th IEEE International Conference on Intelligent Systems and Embedded Design (ISED 2025), organized by NIT Raipur and published in IEEE Xplore (Scopus Indexed). This achievement reflects their strong research commitment and contribution to AI-driven innovation—heartiest congratulations to the team on this milestone 🚀✨.

achievementContents.title: KSV Students Present Research Papers at NASCENT MR 2025 National Conference
achievementContents.description: M. M. Patel Students Research Project Cell, KSV (MMPSRPC), Kadi Sarva Vishwavidyalaya, Gandhinagar, proudly shares that Students Research Lab (SRL) members from Vidush Somany Institute of Technology and Research, Kadi and LDRP ITR participated in the NASCENT MR – National Scientific Conference on Emerging Trends in Multidisciplinary Research 2025 held at Puducherry and organized by Vinayaka Mission's Research Foundation. The students successfully presented their accepted papers.

achievementContents.title: KSV Students Publish Research in Springer LNNS Series at International Conference on Data Science and Security
achievementContents.description: A significant research milestone for the M. M. Patel Students Research Project Cell – Students Research Lab (SRL) as student researchers successfully published their paper titled "Improving Urban Road Safety: Enhancing Pedestrian Safety Through Automated Traffic Signal Control and Law Enforcement" in the Springer Lecture Notes in Networks and Systems (LNNS) book series – Data Science and Security. The research was presented at the International Conference on Data Science, Computation and Security 2024, highlighting innovative approaches to improving urban road safety through intelligent traffic signal control and enforcement mechanisms. Guided by Dr. Himani Trivedi, this achievement reflects the strong research culture, innovation, and application-driven learning nurtured within the Students Research Lab.

activities.title: SRL Students Researchers Team at the IndiaAI Impact Summit 2026
activities.description:
Kadi Sarva Vishwavidyalaya (KSV), Gandhinagar, proudly highlights the distinguished participation of its KSV Students at the IndiaAI Impact Summit 2026 — an international-level AI summit organized under the Digital India initiative by the Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY), Government of India, through INDIAai, held from 16th to 20th February 2026 at Bharat Mandapam, Sushma Swaraj Bhawan & Ambedkar Bhawan, New Delhi.

KSV Students Researchers Team Selected for Casebook on AI and Gender Empowerment
• Janki Chitroda, CE, LDRP-ITR, KSV
• Yashvi Chavda, CE, LDRP-ITR, KSV
• Krishna Bhatt, CE, LDRP-ITR, KSV
Guided By: Dr. Himani Trivedi Ma'am, Head, M. M. Patel Students Research Project Cell, KSV

In parallel with the summit, the KSV Students Researchers Team has been selected for the Casebook on AI and Gender Empowerment initiative, led by the Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY), Government of India, in collaboration with UN Women India, for scalable, gender-responsive AI solutions across the Global South. Our selected KSV students will have the valuable opportunity to present their research work to the global audience of policymakers and innovators, marking national recognition of KSV's impactful research.
activities.brief: KSV students represented at the IndiaAI Impact Summit 2026 and were selected for the international Casebook on AI and Gender Empowerment.

activities.title: Regional AI Impact Conference 2025
activities.description:
M. M. Patel Students Research Project Cell, KSV Students Research Lab Members are attending the Regional AI Impact Conference 2025 🤖, gaining real-time exposure to conversations that define the future of artificial intelligence in India. Surrounded by experts from industry, government, and academia, our students are experiencing how ideas evolve when shared in a space built on innovation and collaboration.

This engagement offers them a chance to observe emerging perspectives, understand the practical depth of AI-driven governance, and connect their classroom learning with larger national developments. Such opportunities help strengthen their analytical thinking and broaden the foundation on which their research ambitions grow.

An enriching experience of meaningful learning, professional growth and thoughtful exploration 📘✨.

activities.title: IndiaSkills Regional Competition 2026
activities.description:
🌟 KSV Students Attended IndiaSkills Regional Competition 2026
📍 Gandhinagar, Gujarat
📅 16th February 2026

Students of Kadi Sarva Vishwavidyalaya (KSV), Gandhinagar from the M. M. Patel Students Research Project Cell, KSV, attended the IndiaSkills Regional Competition 2026, Gandhinagar, Gujarat, as participants.

The competition brought together talented participants from across the Western Region of India, Gujarat, Maharashtra, Rajasthan, and Goa, along with the Union Territories of Dadra & Nagar Haveli and Daman & Diu, who showcased exceptional expertise across diverse professional skill domains.

Domains Showcased
Participants showcased excellence in key areas including Construction, Creative Design, IT & Digital Skills, and Health Services, reflecting strong skill development, innovation, and industry-level precision.

Attending the competition provided them with valuable exposure to industry-level standards and real-world performance expectations. They expressed that witnessing the dedication, technical expertise, and professionalism of the participants was both inspiring and motivating.

The experience enhanced their communication skills, broadened their awareness of national and global platforms, and encouraged them to actively prepare for future competitive opportunities.

activities.title: Research Internship Project Presentations
activities.description:
🎓 Celebrating Excellence in Research! 🎓
We are delighted to share that the Student Research Lab (SRL) at M. M. Patel Students Research Project Cell, KSV hosted a remarkable Research Internship Project Presentation on 29th November 2025.

A total of six internship projects were presented by our talented students, who completed their internships at two prestigious national organizations:
🔬 CSIR Internships (6 Students)
 Our students gained invaluable hands-on research experience at the Council of Scientific and Industrial Research (CSIR), India's largest network of R&D laboratories.
🛡️ NTRO Internships (2 Students)
 Two of our students completed their internships at the National Technical Research Organisation (NTRO), a premier technical intelligence agency of India.

 Palak Vanpariya, CE, LDRP-ITR, KSV
 Dhrumi Patel, CE, LDRP-ITR, KSV
 Kritika Thakkar, CE, LDRP-ITR, KSV
 Princy Patel, CE, LDRP-ITR, KSV

activities.description:
MMPSRPC Students Research Lab: A Space for Meaningful Work
The M. M. Patel Students Research Project Cell, KSV (MMPSRPC) Students Research Lab has become a place where students go beyond simply completing academic tasks and begin developing a strong research mindset through exploration, thoughtful discussions, and consistent guidance.

Inside this lab, students are not just creating projects; they are nurturing a research-driven approach where ideas develop with purpose, conversations bring clarity, and collaboration happens naturally as students learn from one another’s strengths.

For students across KSV, the research lab represents the moment when a simple idea transforms into meaningful, well-structured work.

activities.title: Bridging Theory & Practice – A Research Talk
activities.description:
The session "Bridging Theory & Practice – A Research Talk" was conducted on 24th February 2026 at the Students Research Lab, KSV, organized by MMPSRPC in collaboration with IEEE KSV SB.

The session was delivered by Dr. Mukti Advani, Senior Principal Scientist at CSIR–CRRI, who shared insights on translating research into real-world impact, particularly in road engineering, infrastructure, and safety. She highlighted the role of AI/ML in road safety through initiatives like iRASTE, enabling real-time monitoring, risk prediction, and data-driven decision-making.

The event concluded with an interactive discussion where participants proposed practical, research-oriented solutions.
activities.brief: A research talk by Dr. Mukti Advani (CSIR–CRRI) on translating research into real-world impact in road safety and AI/ML applications.

researchProjects.title: AI for Gender Empowerment: Designing Inclusive and Bias-Aware Intelligent Systems
researchProjects.description: This research explores how Artificial Intelligence can serve as a catalyst for gender empowerment by identifying, analyzing, and addressing structural inequalities that persist within digital ecosystems. The study investigates the intersection of AI technologies, social inclusion, and gender equity, highlighting how intelligent systems can either mitigate or amplify existing disparities depending on design, data, and deployment choices. The work emphasizes gender-responsive AI frameworks, focusing on ethical model development, bias awareness, accessibility, and representation. Through practical case analysis, the research demonstrates how AI-driven solutions can enhance opportunities for women and underrepresented groups in areas such as education, decision-making, digital participation, and resource accessibility. It also evaluates challenges including algorithmic bias, data imbalance, and socio-technical barriers that influence equitable outcomes. Ultimately, the study advocates for responsible AI innovation where fairness, inclusivity, and human-centric design guide technological progress, ensuring that AI evolves as an instrument of empowerment rather than exclusion.

researchProjects.title: AI and Data-Driven Approaches for Predictive Healthcare and Risk Pattern Analysis
researchProjects.description:
Paper 1: SHAP-Enhanced Outbreak Forecasting: Interpretable Multi-Modal Learning for Waterborne Disease Prediction
Presents an interpretable AI framework combining multi-modal data and SHAP analysis to improve transparency and accuracy in predicting waterborne disease outbreaks.

Paper 2: Enhancing Data Mining Techniques for Identifying Health Risk Patterns in Underserved Populations
Explores advanced data mining approaches to uncover hidden health risk patterns, supporting early intervention and informed decision-making in underserved communities.

researchProjects.title: Sustainability and Ethical Innovation: Research Contributions from KSV at the 9th NYC Green School Conference 2025
researchProjects.description:
Faculty and student researchers from LDRP Institute of Technology & Research, Kadi Sarva Vishwavidyalaya (KSV), Gandhinagar, proudly represented the university at the 9th NYC Green School Conference 2025 held at Cornell University, New York. The conference provided an international platform for knowledge exchange and interdisciplinary dialogue on sustainability, ethical technology, and future-ready educational practices. The KSV delegation presented research spanning sustainable construction materials, ethical AI-based learning frameworks, and intelligent sustainability diagnostics for educational institutions. These contributions highlighted KSV’s commitment to impactful research, global academic collaboration, and innovation aligned with sustainable development goals. This initiative was made possible under the expert guidance and unwavering support of Dr. Jinal Joshi, Director, Department of International Relations, SVKM, whose mentorship played a pivotal role in facilitating this global opportunity. Participation at this prestigious global forum reflects the university’s growing international research footprint and dedication to fostering socially responsible, technology-driven solutions.

Paper 1: Hemp in Building: A Natural Step Toward Sustainable Construction
Focuses on the use of hemp as a sustainable construction material, exploring its properties, environmental benefits, and potential for green building applications.

Paper 2: Ethical AI and Gamified Pedagogy for Sustainable Classrooms
Explores the integration of ethical AI and gamification strategies to create engaging, sustainable, and inclusive learning environments.

Paper 3: EcoAudit AI: Sustainability Diagnostics & Resource Optimization in Smart Educational Institutions
Introduces an AI-driven system for sustainability audits and resource optimization, aimed at enhancing energy efficiency and environmental practices in educational institutions.

researchProjects.title: KSV Student Researchers Selected for Finale at National Poster Competition on AI Powered Research and Innovation
researchProjects.description:
Students from LDRP Institute of Technology & Research, Kadi Sarva Vishwavidyalaya (KSV), Gandhinagar, under the guidance of the M. M. Patel Students Research Project Cell (MMPSRPC), have achieved a notable academic milestone by securing selection for the finale of the National Poster Competition on AI Powered Research and Innovation, organized by Gujarat Technological University (GTU), Ahmedabad. The competition, scheduled for 19th & 20th September 2025, brings together promising student researchers presenting innovative, technology-driven solutions addressing real-world challenges through Artificial Intelligence and data-centric methodologies.

Team 1: Bridging the Disconnect: Holistic Student Dropout Analysis in Schools through Data-Driven Machine Learning
This work presents a data-driven machine learning framework designed to analyze and predict student dropout risks through a holistic evaluation of academic, behavioral, and socio-economic indicators. By integrating multi-dimensional educational data, the study identifies hidden patterns and early warning signals associated with disengagement and attrition. The model emphasizes interpretability and practical usability, enabling educators and institutions to design timely, targeted interventions that improve retention, student support strategies, and overall learning outcomes.

Team 2: Tracking Air Pollution using INSAT Satellite and Ground Data Fusion
This study explores an integrated environmental monitoring approach that combines INSAT satellite observations with ground-based measurements to enhance air pollution assessment and tracking. Through data fusion techniques, the research improves spatial and temporal understanding of pollutant distribution, overcoming limitations of isolated sensing methods. The proposed framework supports more accurate pollution analysis, facilitating informed decision-making, environmental planning, and policy development aimed at sustainable urban management and public health protection.
